mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales signals robust consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward IC over a 3-year rolling window is below 0.03, or the correlation between RSAFS level and 20-day returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals cyclical weakness in the labor market, reducing consumer spending and confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, SPX 20-day returns are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate of negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is extremely low and consumer prices are rising, negative real interest rates compress discount rates and encourage equity investment, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with rising inflation
failure_condition: During trigger episodes, 20-day SPX returns have a median below zero or rank IC is not positive at the 10% significance level over a 2-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion phase, strong GDP growth signals overheating, prompting expectations of policy tightening that eventually depress equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a long horizon.
failure_condition: If 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of episodes following high GDP readings (top quartile), or if the negative correlation between GDP level and forward returns fails to exceed -0.10 over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production indicates imminent supply-side weakness, reducing output and corporate profits, causing negative S&P 500 returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: If 5-day returns are negative in less than 60% of trigger events, or the average return is not significantly negative (one-sample t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts are elevated and the federal funds rate is very low, it suggests an asset bubble fueled by cheap credit, which becomes vulnerable to a correction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
regime_hint: asset price inflation with loose monetary policy
failure_condition: If 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the average cumulative return is positive at 95% confidence.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary easing regime (very low Fed funds rate), the discount rate effect dominates, boosting equity valuations regardless of other fundamentals, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the near term.
failure_condition: If the rank IC of the inverse of FEDFUNDS on 20-day returns is not positive and >0.05 over any 2-year walk-forward period, or if returns are negative more than 50% of the time during easing regimes.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in the consumer price index above 0.5% month-over-month signals rising inflationary pressure, which may prompt the Fed to tighten policy, reducing equity attractiveness and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over one week.
failure_condition: If 5-day returns are negative in less than 55% of trigger months, or the average return is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.10).
